overview: The Vanguard All-Equity ETF Portfolio (VEQT.TO) is a 100% equity all-in-one ETF that provides globally diversified stock exposure with zero bonds. The approximate allocation is ~43% U.S., ~30% Canada, ~18% International Developed, and ~9% Emerging Markets. Note: VEQT has a higher Canadian home bias (~30%) compared to XEQT (~25%). VEQT holds underlying Vanguard index ETFs and automatically rebalances to maintain its target allocation. With an MER of 0.20%, it is designed for long-term investors with high risk tolerance who want maximum equity exposure in a single ticker. No fixed income, no bonds — pure equity growth.
bullCase: 100% equity with global diversification — maximum long-term growth potential.
All-in-one solution at 0.20% MER with automatic rebalancing across 4 regions.
Ideal for young investors with long time horizons who can tolerate volatility.
bearCase: 100% equity means full exposure to market downturns — can drop 30-50% in crashes.
No fixed-income cushion — not suitable for conservative or near-retirement investors.
Currency exposure to USD, EUR, JPY, and emerging market currencies adds volatility.
analystSummary: VEQT is the most popular all-equity ETF in Canada. Its 0.20% MER buys you ~40% US, ~30% Canada, ~20% International, ~10% Emerging Markets — all automatically rebalanced. Pure equity, no bonds.
seoDescription: Explore VEQT ETF analysis — Vanguard All-Equity Portfolio with 100% global equity at 0.20% MER. Is it right for your portfolio?
